reviewer: reviewer (sonnet), independent of the fixer that authored 14c2a72
review_method: Diffed the single fix commit against its actual parent (14c2a72^, i.e. 53f0e08) rather than current main, for the same base-drift reason as cluster-F (branch cut from 53f0e08; main has since gained cluster-a/c/system-003 merges that show up as spurious deletions in a naive main..branch diff). `git diff 14c2a72^..14c2a72 --stat` shows the real fix touches 9 frontend files, +1161/-687 (of which 840 lines are a like-for-like move of pre-existing settings/page.tsx logic into settings-client.tsx, verified by direct diff against the old file, plus a genuine new billing section).

honest_copy.evidence: apps/web/src/app/pricing/page.tsx: removed the per-plan '{plan.modelTier} model tier' subtitle line (old lines ~148-150) and added MODEL_TIER_CLAIM_RE = /model tier|model access/i to filter any feature bullet claiming per-plan model differentiation before rendering (page.tsx:~29,~225-231). Verified against actual backend copy in apps/api/app/routers/billing.py:36-61 (_PLAN_FEATURES): all 4 current model-differentiation strings ('Light model tier', 'Standard model tier', 'Advanced model tier', 'Full model access') match the filter regex, so none render. A new honest header states 'Every plan uses the same AI models -- plans differ by monthly agent-run quota and feature access, not model quality' (data-testid=pricing-model-honesty-note). Grepped both pricing/page.tsx and settings-client.tsx for 'Anthropic|Claude|GPT|OpenAI' -- zero matches, confirming no vendor/model-serving claim was introduced.
honest_copy.note: This is a display-layer filter, not a backend copy fix (backend still returns modelTier + the model-tier feature strings in the API response). Acceptable given the task's explicit 'Frontend-only per assignment scope; no backend changes' boundary (stated in the commit message and consistent with this review's own instruction to use only vitest/tsc/build, not pytest), but the underlying misleading backend copy is only masked, not removed at the source -- flagged as a residual, non-blocking root-cause gap for a future backend-scoped cluster.

plan_rendered.evidence: New 'Billing & Subscription' section in settings-client.tsx (~lines 642-738, moved wholesale from a like-for-like diff check against the old page.tsx which had none of this) fetches GET /billing/subscription via the pre-existing fetchSubscription() (apps/web/src/lib/api/billing.ts:88-94, unmodified/pre-existing -- confirms the endpoint-wiring gap was real: the function existed but nothing called it from Settings) and renders plan name, status, cancelAtPeriodEnd, billing interval, run quota (runsUsed/runsAllowed with a progress bar), spend quota, and renewal date (data-testid='billing-plan-name' etc.). settings.ts sections.ts appends an 8th 'Billing & Subscription' nav entry after the 7 wireframe-pinned entries (verified via diff -- inserted last, not reordering the original 7, and settings-subnav.test.ts explicitly guards against reordering).

manage_sub_wired.evidence: Confirmed POST /billing/portal exists at apps/api/app/routers/billing.py:507-529 (create_portal), returns {'portalUrl': session['url']}. settings-client.tsx manageSubscription() (~lines 86-116) calls openBillingPortal() (pre-existing lib/api/billing.ts:96-104) and does window.location.href = portalUrl on success. On error: 401 -> silent (shared client already redirects to /login); 409 (no Stripe customer, confirmed this is the correct status for 'no billing profile' per billing.py's design) -> honest fallback message using AETHER_SUPPORT_EMAIL via the same getOperatorLegalConfig() pattern as terms/privacy ('Email {supportEmail} to manage or cancel...' or, if unset, 'A support contact hasn't been published...'); 503 -> 'Billing management isn't configured on this deployment yet' + support-email fallback; 429 -> Retry-After-aware message via formatRetryAfter(); else generic honest failure message. No fake-success path exists for any error branch -- verified by reading every branch of the if/else chain.

checkout_errors.evidence: apps/web/src/lib/api/client.ts:27-45 ApiError now carries retryAfterSeconds parsed from the 'Retry-After' response header; apiRequest reads it on any non-OK response. pricing/page.tsx handleSubscribe distinguishes 400 ('This plan isn't available for purchase yet...') from 429 ('Too many checkout attempts -- please try again in about {formatRetryAfter(...)}') from the pre-existing 503 and generic-fallback branches -- 4 distinct messages, not collapsed into one. Confirmed against apps/api/app/routers/billing.py: /billing/checkout raises 400 when _resolve_price_id returns falsy (line 138-141, 'This plan is not yet available for purchase (no Stripe price configured)') and 429 with a real Retry-After header from limiter.retry_after(user_id) (lines 126-130) -- the frontend's error taxonomy matches the backend's actual status codes, this isn't invented client-side branching for statuses the API never sends. formatRetryAfter(90) verified by direct execution to return '2 minutes', matching the test's assertion.
checkout_errors.session_aware_cta: Free-tier CTA reads window.localStorage.getItem('aether_token') in a useEffect (page.tsx:71-73) and renders 'Go to dashboard' -> /dashboard when a token is present, else 'Get started free' -> /signup (page.tsx:~232-240). Both branches independently tested (pricing/__tests__/page.test.tsx: two tests, one with a stored token, one without).

tests_meaningful.evidence: Pricing tests assert absence of 'model tier'/'model access' text AND presence of the honest 'same AI models' copy (not just one side); the 400/429 tests use real ApiError instances constructed with the actual status codes and assert the rendered message differs from the generic 'Could not start checkout' fallback, and the 429 test asserts the literal computed '2 minutes' string, not just that any string is shown. Settings billing tests assert real fetched plan name/status/quota numbers appear (15/100), that clicking 'Manage subscription' actually calls openBillingPortal and follows window.location.href to the mocked portal URL (real behavioral wiring, not just a button existing), and the 409 test asserts the fallback message contains 'billing profile|contact|support' AND explicitly asserts .not.toMatch(/success/i) -- ruling out a fake-success message. settings-subnav test asserts SECTIONS.length===8 and the last entry is exactly {id:'billing', label:'Billing & Subscription'}, guarding both the addition and non-reordering of the original 7.

vitest_result.tsc: pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -> no output, exit clean
vitest_result.build: pnpm run build -> ✓ Compiled successfully, ✓ Generating static pages (28/28); route table shows '/dashboard/settings' marked ƒ (Dynamic, server-rendered on demand), confirming the new server-wrapper page.tsx (reads AETHER_SUPPORT_EMAIL via getOperatorLegalConfig() at request time) took effect; '/pricing' remains ○ (static, client-rendered, as before -- unaffected since it doesn't need server-side env reads)

reasons: PASS: MV-pricing-002 fixed at the display layer with a filter proven comprehensive against the actual current backend feature-copy list; honest replacement header added; no vendor/model-name claim introduced.

PASS: MV-pricing-003/MV-settings-003 fixed with a genuine new Billing & Subscription section wired to real, pre-existing, previously-unused API client functions (fetchSubscription, openBillingPortal) hitting endpoints independently confirmed to exist in apps/api/app/routers/billing.py (GET /billing/subscription line 442, POST /billing/portal line 507); 409/503/429 all produce an honest, distinct fallback message, never a fake success.
PASS: MV-pricing-004 checkout error taxonomy (400/429 distinct from 503/generic, Retry-After honored) verified to match the real backend status codes and headers, not invented client-side guesses.
PASS: MV-pricing-005 Free CTA session-awareness verified with both authenticated and unauthenticated test cases.

PASS: settings page.tsx -> settings-client.tsx refactor verified via direct diff against the pre-fix file to contain ONLY the billing addition plus the server/client split -- no unrelated behavior silently changed in the 670 lines of moved logic.
PASS: 306/306 vitest, clean tsc, clean production build with /dashboard/settings confirmed dynamic (ƒ) in the build's route table.

No prohibited patterns found: the one 'eslint-disable-next-line import/first' line in the settings page test diff is a pre-existing, unchanged context line (confirmed no +/- prefix) from before this commit, not newly introduced; no @ts-ignore/as any/Math.random/TODO/FIXME added by this commit.
Scope discipline: touches only apps/web files as required (frontend-only); no backend files modified.

Non-blocking residual gap noted above: MV-pricing-002's fix filters misleading copy at render time rather than removing it at the backend source (_PLAN_FEATURES / modelTier in apps/api/app/routers/billing.py still contains the false model-differentiation claim) -- acceptable under this cluster's declared frontend-only scope, but should be tracked for a backend-scoped follow-up so the API response itself stops asserting a false claim to any other consumer.

Non-blocking observation: apps/web/src/lib/config/legal.ts is byte-identical to the file independently added by fix/mv-cluster-f (both cut from the same 53f0e08 base) -- a trivial same-content overlap for the orchestrator to be aware of at merge time, not a defect in this branch.
